concerns.text: Task T015 instructs to 'Implement iteration_count logic... CRITICAL: Do NOT exclude commits containing Copilot'. This contradicts the Spec (FR-002) which mandates exclusion. The task does not explicitly state to OVERRRIDE FR-002 or cite the Plan.md resolution. An implementer following the spec text will fail; one following the task text violates the spec. The task must explicitly reference the Plan.md conflict resolution to be executable.

Tasks T030 (GLMM) and T031 (ZINB) mandate specific statistical models. However, Spec FR-003 only authorizes 'linear regression analysis'. The tasks do not cite the Plan.md section that authorizes this methodological expansion. Without this explicit link, the tasks are unexecutable as they contradict the authorized scope in the spec.

Task T001 'Create project structure per implementation plan' is too coarse. It does not name the specific directories or files to be created. The Plan.md lists a specific tree structure; this task should be split into atomic tasks for each directory/file creation or explicitly list the target paths (e.g., 'Create data/raw/, data/derived/, docs/output/').

Task T002 'Initialize Python 3.11 project with requirements.txt dependencies' is too coarse. It does not list the dependencies. The Plan.md lists specific dependencies (pandas, requests, etc.). The task must either list these explicitly or reference the specific section in Plan.md to be executable.

Tasks T042-T044 (Phase 6) require adding specific theoretical sections (Holland's work, distributed cognition) to the report. The Spec (US3) and Success Criteria (SC-005) do not mandate these specific citations or sections. The tasks do not reference the Plan.md or Reviewer Feedback that authorized this scope. They are unexecutable without an explicit source for these specific content requirements.
